Title: Overexpression of cytoplasmic C4 Flaveria bidentis carbonic anhydrase in C3 Arabidopsis thaliana increases amino acids, photosynthetic potential, and biomass

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides anaplerotic carbon skeletons for amino acid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Uses bicarbonate supplied by overexpressed FbβCA3 to generate oxaloacetate/malate flux feeding TCA-linked amino acid synthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PEPC is the immediate endogenous metabolic sink for the added bicarbonate generated by FbβCA3. 2. Increased malate and amino acids were inferred to result from increased substrate supply to PEPC. 3. In vitro PEPC activity with exogenous bicarbonate was unchanged, indicating no direct enhancement of intrinsic PEPC catalytic capacity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PEPC enzyme assay; metabolite profiling; pathway inference from transgenic phenotype
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be paired with bicarbonate-supplying enzymes in metabolic engineering, but was not directly manipulated here
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene/enzyme is functionally important in the paper’s model but is not the primary manipulated gene in the Results.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Photosynthetic carbon assimilation / anaplerotic carbon metabolism linked to amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic CO2 hydration supplying HCO3− for endogenous PEPC and downstream TCA-linked amino acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reversible hydration of CO2 to bicarbonate in the cytosol, increasing HCO3− availability for endogenous phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Aspartate; asparagine; glutamate; glutamine; sugars; starch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports higher protein accumulation, photosynthetic capacity, growth, biomass, and improved water-use efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of TCA-linked amino acids and carbohydrates, increases photosynthetic CO2 assimilation and biomass, and improves water-use efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Constitutive overexpression of FbβCA3 in Arabidopsis increased CA transcript, protein abundance, and enzyme activity. 2. Increased cytosolic bicarbonate supply elevated malate and fumarate and strongly increased aspartate and asparagine, with smaller increases in glutamate and glutamine. 3. Transgenics showed higher chlorophyll, higher photosynthetic electron transport and CO2 assimilation, more sugars and starch, and greater dry biomass with reduced stomatal conductance and transpiration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis; PCR and Southern blot confirmation; qRT-PCR; immunoblot; CA enzyme assay; GC-MS metabolite profiling; gas exchange; chlorophyll fluorescence; biomass measurement
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive transgene expression in 4-week-old leaves/rosettes of Arabidopsis transformants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants grown at 21 ± 1°C under 14 h light/10 h dark, cool-white fluorescent light (100 μmol photons m−2 s−1); gas exchange also tested under ambient CO2, 21% or 2% O2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR targeted expression analysis; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as an important control point for bicarbonate supply/anaplerotic support, but not directly established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dark respiration increased ~12%–16%; no reduction in photorespiration compensation point reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to improve photosynthesis, amino acid content, carbohydrate accumulation, biomass, and water-use efficiency in C3 crops; likely useful in stacking with other C4 or photosynthetic engineering traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PEPC in vitro activity itself was unchanged when bicarbonate was supplied exogenously, indicating the major effect is increased substrate availability rather than altered PEPC abundance/activity.
